deep_dive.title: A Geometric Account of Activation Steering through Angle-Norm Decomposition
deep_dive.abstract: Linear activation steering has gained popularity as a simple and empirically effective way to control language model behavior. More recently, spherical steering paradigms have been proposed to address limitations of additive interventions, often motivated by the assumption that hidden-state norm does not carry concept-relevant information. In this work, we revisit this assumption through a controlled empirical study designed to disentangle the roles of angular and radial components. We show that steering methods differ mainly in how they couple two geometric effects: changing a token's angular alignment with a concept direction and changing its hidden-state norm. Across seven language models, we find that concepts are represented primarily in angular structure, supporting the motivation for spherical methods, but that norm remains important for the stability and downstream effects of steering. Our results explain why interventions with similar concept-level effects can behave differently, and suggest that activation steering should be parameterized by interpretable angular and radial components of the intervention, rather than by a single additive coefficient that entangles these two effects.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接研究激活引导机制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接研究激活引导机制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接研究激活引导机制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper geometrically decomposes activation steering into angle and norm effects, showing that concepts are primarily angular, providing fundamental insights for steering-based methods.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有激活引导方法依赖线性干预但缺乏几何理解。本文通过角度-范数分解揭示了激活引导的几何效应，发现概念主要由角度维度编码，不同引导方法的差异在于耦合角度和范数变化。该研究为基于激活引导的安全数据生成等应用提供了理论基础。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有激活引导方法依赖线性干预但缺乏几何理解。本文通过角度-范数分解揭示了激活引导的几何效应，发现概念主要由角度维度编码，不同引导方法的差异在于耦合角度和范数变化。该研究为基于激活引导的安全数据生成等应用提供了理论基础。

deep_dive.title: SafeGene: Reusable Adapters for Transferable Safety Alignment
deep_dive.abstract: Open-weight LLMs are increasingly fine-tuned into customized assistants, but downstream fine-tuning can weaken safety alignment and make models more vulnerable to malicious prompts, even when the training data is not intentionally harmful. This creates a recurring safety recovery problem as target models are repeatedly updated with new task data or user interactions. We propose SafeGene, a reusable safety-adapter module designed for cross-task reuse within each architecture-compatible model family. Rather than treating safety recovery as a model-specific repair step, SafeGene treats safety capability as an independent, reusable adapter representation decoupled from task-specific updates. This representation is obtained from aligned--degraded model discrepancies, refined into task-transferable safety vectors through data-aware layer selection, and expressed in each downstream task-adapted model via few-shot layer-wise coefficient recalibration. Experiments across multiple model families, downstream tasks, and safety judges show that SafeGene-enhanced models reduce harmful response rates while maintaining downstream performance, outperforming representative safe adaptation methods in safety--utility trade-off.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: reusable safety adapter to recover alignment after fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 可重用安全适配器，在微调后恢复对齐
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 可重用安全适配器，在微调后恢复对齐
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 可重用安全适配器，在微调后恢复对齐
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SafeGene introduces a reusable safety adapter module decoupled from task-specific updates to recover alignment lost during fine-tuning without retraining, enabling cross-task reuse.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对下游微调导致LLM安全对齐弱化的问题，SafeGene提出将安全能力作为独立可重用适配器表示，通过对齐模型与退化模型的差异来学习适配器，可在不同任务间复用，避免了每次更新后重新修复安全性的开销，有效缓解安全退化。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对下游微调导致LLM安全对齐弱化的问题，SafeGene提出将安全能力作为独立可重用适配器表示，通过对齐模型与退化模型的差异来学习适配器，可在不同任务间复用，避免了每次更新后重新修复安全性的开销，有效缓解安全退化。
